name: H-IIA
first: Japan's longest-serving operational launcher — flew Hayabusa, Akatsuki, and Hope Probe
description: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries' two-stage workhorse, in service da 2001 through retirement in 2024. Cryogenic core stage with 2-4 solid boosters. Notable interplanetary missioni: Hayabusa (2003) and Hayabusa2 (2014) a asteroids; Akatsuki (2010) a Venus; the UAE's Hope Probe (2020) a Marte; SLIM (2023) a the Luna. Replaced by the H3.
